100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 23, 1926, the international community was abuzz as negotiations continued over the future of the German economy, with reparations at the forefront of discussions. Meanwhile, in Britain, the government faced mounting pressure from labor strikes, with workers demanding better wages and conditions. In the United States, excitement surrounded the upcoming summer festivities, including the planned opening of the Chicago Century of Progress Exposition, showcasing advancements in technology and industry.
